task_description:
Screen these abstracts for a systematic review on ML methods for brain tumour detection or classification from MRI. Produce a ranked top-5 shortlist.

INCLUDE only papers that present or evaluate an ML model that detects or classifies brain tumour type, grade, or malignancy from MRI. Papers about brain tumour segmentation without classification, treatment planning, prognosis, genomic subtype prediction from MRI, or non-brain tumours should be EXCLUDE.

For each INCLUDE paper: assign rank 1 (best) to 5 (fifth-best) and write one justification sentence (max 200 chars).

Quality rubric: 1=no method; 2=vague; 3=clear metrics; 4=multiple baselines, external validation, and ablation.

papers.title: 3D ResNet for WHO 2021 Brain Tumour Grade Classification from Multi-Parametric MRI
papers.abstract: WHO 2021 brain tumour grading integrates morphology, IDH mutation, and 1p/19q codeletion. We train a 3D ResNet on 1,400 multi-parametric MRI studies (T1, T2, T1ce, FLAIR) to classify glioma WHO grade 2, 3, and 4 without biopsy. Three-way macro-AUC 0.91 is achieved, outperforming radiologist consensus, a 2D slice CNN, and a radiomic SVM baseline. An external validation cohort and ablation of imaging modalities are provided.
papers.topic_hint: 3D ResNet, WHO grading, brain tumour, multi-parametric MRI, glioma
papers.methodology_hint: 3D ResNet, 1400 mpMRI, macro-AUC 0.91, 3 baselines, external validation, modality ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: 3D ResNet for glioma WHO grade classification macro-AUC 0.91, 3 baselines, external validation, ablation.

papers.title: Swin Transformer for Brain Tumour Segmentation on BraTS 2021
papers.abstract: We develop a Swin Transformer-based U-Net for brain tumour segmentation on BraTS 2021. Dice 0.93 for whole tumour, 0.89 for tumour core, and 0.86 for enhancing tumour are achieved, outperforming CNN baselines. The model produces voxel-level segmentation masks, not tumour type or grade classification.
papers.topic_hint: Swin Transformer, brain tumour segmentation, BraTS 2021
papers.methodology_hint: Swin Transformer, BraTS 2021, segmentation Dice — no classification
papers.claimed_contribution: Swin Transformer brain tumour segmentation Dice 0.93, BraTS 2021, no classification.

papers.title: ViT for Distinguishing Primary CNS Lymphoma from Glioblastoma on MRI
papers.abstract: CNS lymphoma and GBM have overlapping MRI appearances but require different treatment. We fine-tune ViT-B/16 on 820 MRI studies to binary-classify PCNSL versus GBM. AUC 0.92 is achieved, outperforming ResNet-50 and a neuroradiologist panel in a blinded read. An ablation study confirms that contrast-enhanced T1 is the most discriminative sequence.
papers.topic_hint: ViT, CNS lymphoma, GBM, MRI classification, differential diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: ViT-B/16, 820 MRI studies, AUC 0.92, ResNet-50 and neuroradiologist baselines, ablation of sequences
papers.claimed_contribution: ViT for PCNSL vs GBM differential on MRI, AUC 0.92, radiologist comparison, sequence ablation.

papers.title: Graph Neural Network for Predicting MGMT Methylation Status from MRI Features
papers.abstract: MGMT methylation status predicts response to temozolomide chemotherapy in GBM. We extract radiomic and deep features from MRI and train a GNN to predict MGMT status. AUC 0.76 is achieved. This predicts a molecular biomarker from MRI, not tumour type or histological grade.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, MGMT methylation, GBM, MRI, molecular prediction
papers.methodology_hint: GNN, MGMT prediction, AUC 0.76 — molecular biomarker not tumour classification
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN for MGMT prediction from MRI AUC 0.76, molecular biomarker not tumour classification.

papers.title: Radiotherapy Dose Planning for Glioblastoma Using Deep Learning Autocontouring
papers.abstract: Automated GTV and CTV delineation for GBM radiotherapy planning is time-consuming. We train a U-Net on 380 treatment planning MRI studies to autocontour structures. Dice 0.87 for GTV is achieved. This is a radiotherapy planning tool; no tumour type or grade classification is performed.
papers.topic_hint: U-Net, radiotherapy planning, GBM, autocontouring
papers.methodology_hint: U-Net, autocontouring Dice 0.87, radiation planning — not tumour classification
papers.claimed_contribution: U-Net for GBM radiotherapy autocontouring Dice 0.87, not tumour classification.

papers.title: EfficientNet for Distinguishing Benign from Malignant Brain Tumour in Paediatric MRI
papers.abstract: Paediatric brain tumours include low-grade gliomas, medulloblastomas, and ependymomas. We fine-tune EfficientNet-B3 on 1,100 paediatric MRI studies to binary classify benign versus malignant tumours. AUC 0.89 is achieved, with external validation on a children's hospital database and comparison against a paediatric neuroradiology panel.
papers.topic_hint: EfficientNet, paediatric brain tumour, benign vs malignant, MRI
papers.methodology_hint: EfficientNet-B3, 1100 paediatric MRI, AUC 0.89, external validation, neuroradiology comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: EfficientNet for paediatric brain tumour benign/malignant classification AUC 0.89, external validation.

papers.title: Survival Analysis for Overall Survival in GBM Using Deep MRI Features
papers.abstract: Predicting GBM overall survival supports treatment stratification. We train a deep survival model on deep radiomic features extracted from post-resection MRI of 640 GBM patients. C-index 0.67 is achieved. This predicts survival after diagnosis; tumour classification is not performed.
papers.topic_hint: survival analysis, GBM, overall survival, MRI features, prognosis
papers.methodology_hint: deep survival, 640 GBM, C-index 0.67, prognosis not classification
papers.claimed_contribution: Deep survival model for GBM OS prediction C-index 0.67, prognosis not classification.

papers.title: Attention-Guided CNN for Meningioma Grade Classification from Contrast-Enhanced MRI
papers.abstract: Meningioma grading is important for surgical decision-making. We develop an attention-guided CNN trained on 920 contrast-enhanced MRI studies to classify meningioma grade (WHO I, II, III). Macro-F1 0.84 is achieved, outperforming a vanilla ResNet-50 and a radiomics SVM. The model attends to enhancement pattern and brain invasion zones, confirmed by post-surgical histology.
papers.topic_hint: attention CNN, meningioma grading, contrast-enhanced MRI, WHO grade
papers.methodology_hint: attention CNN, 920 CE-MRI, macro-F1 0.84, ResNet-50 and SVM baselines, histology correlation
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention CNN for meningioma WHO grading macro-F1 0.84, 2 baselines, histology correlation.

papers.title: MRI Reconstruction with Deep Unrolling for Accelerated Brain Imaging
papers.abstract: Deep unrolling networks reduce MRI acquisition time by reconstructing undersampled k-space data. We achieve 4x acceleration for T2-FLAIR brain MRI with SSIM 0.93 versus full sampling. This is an image reconstruction method; no brain tumour diagnosis is performed.
papers.topic_hint: deep unrolling, MRI reconstruction, k-space, acceleration
papers.methodology_hint: deep unrolling, SSIM 0.93, reconstruction — not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Deep unrolling MRI reconstruction at 4x acceleration SSIM 0.93, not tumour diagnosis.

papers.title: Contrastive Pre-training on BraTS and TCGA-GBM for Tumour Type Classification
papers.abstract: Self-supervised pre-training on large brain MRI archives enables label-efficient tumour classification. We apply SimSiam contrastive pre-training on 8,000 multi-parametric MRI volumes from BraTS and TCGA-GBM before fine-tuning for tumour type classification (GBM, LGG, metastasis). Three-way AUC 0.93 is achieved using only 50 labelled scans per class, outperforming ImageNet and MoCo pre-training. An ablation of pre-training objectives is provided.
papers.topic_hint: contrastive pre-training, BraTS, brain tumour classification, SimSiam, label-efficient
papers.methodology_hint: SimSiam pre-training, 8k volumes, AUC 0.93, ImageNet and MoCo baselines, ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: SimSiam pre-training for brain tumour type classification AUC 0.93, 2 baselines, label-efficient ablation.

papers.title: Federated Learning for Multi-Site Brain MRI Abnormality Classification
papers.abstract: We implement federated learning across 12 neuroradiology sites to classify MRI brain abnormalities including tumour presence, stroke, and white matter lesions. AUC 0.89 for tumour detection is achieved. We note the primary focus is federated training methodology; the tumour detection component uses a standard architecture.
papers.topic_hint: federated learning, brain MRI, abnormality classification, tumour detection
papers.methodology_hint: federated learning, 12 sites, AUC 0.89, methodology paper — tumour classification present
papers.claimed_contribution: Federated brain MRI abnormality classifier AUC 0.89 for tumour detection, methodology contribution.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Automated Hippocampal Volume Measurement in Dementia
papers.abstract: Hippocampal atrophy is a biomarker for Alzheimer's disease. We train a U-Net for hippocampal segmentation from structural MRI, achieving Dice 0.92. Downstream volume measurements are compared with FreeSurfer. This is brain segmentation for dementia biomarker quantification, not brain tumour classification.
papers.topic_hint: U-Net, hippocampal segmentation, Alzheimer's, dementia biomarker, MRI
papers.methodology_hint: U-Net, segmentation Dice 0.92, dementia biomarker — not brain tumour
papers.claimed_contribution: U-Net for hippocampal segmentation Dice 0.92 for dementia biomarker, not tumour diagnosis.

papers.title: Multi-Modal Transformer for Brain Metastasis Detection and Primary Site Classification
papers.abstract: Brain metastases require identification and primary site attribution. We develop a multi-modal transformer fusing gadolinium-enhanced MRI and clinical metadata to simultaneously detect brain metastases and classify their primary site (lung, breast, melanoma). AUC 0.90 for primary site classification is achieved, outperforming single-modality CNN and a clinical staging model. Prospective pilot validation on 120 new patients is provided.
papers.topic_hint: multi-modal transformer, brain metastasis, primary site classification, MRI
papers.methodology_hint: multi-modal transformer, AUC 0.90, CNN and clinical staging baselines, prospective pilot
papers.claimed_contribution: Multi-modal transformer for brain metastasis primary site classification AUC 0.90, prospective pilot.

papers.title: Genetic Algorithm Optimisation for MRI Scan Protocol Design
papers.abstract: MRI scan protocols balance image quality and acquisition time. We use genetic algorithms to optimise acquisition parameter combinations for brain MRI. Signal-to-noise ratio improves by 12% with no increase in scan time. No brain disease classification is performed.
papers.topic_hint: genetic algorithm, MRI protocol optimisation, acquisition parameters, SNR
papers.methodology_hint: genetic algorithm, MRI parameter optimisation — not disease classification
papers.claimed_contribution: Genetic algorithm for MRI protocol optimisation, SNR +12%, no disease classification.

papers.title: XGBoost for IDH Mutation Classification in Glioma from MRI Radiomic Features
papers.abstract: IDH mutation status is the primary molecular classifier in WHO 2021 glioma classification. We extract 200 radiomic features from T1, T2, and FLAIR MRI and train XGBoost to classify IDH-mutant versus IDH-wildtype glioma. AUC 0.82 is achieved. Only logistic regression is compared as a baseline; no external validation is included.
papers.topic_hint: XGBoost, IDH mutation, glioma, radiomics, molecular classification
papers.methodology_hint: XGBoost, 200 radiomic features, AUC 0.82, only LR baseline, no external validation
papers.claimed_contribution: XGBoost for IDH mutation classification from MRI radiomics AUC 0.82, limited validation.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Predicting Pseudo-Progression vs True Progression in GBM After Chemoradiation
papers.abstract: Distinguishing pseudo-progression from true progression on MRI guides treatment decisions after chemoradiation. We train a CNN on 540 post-treatment MRI studies to classify pseudo-progression versus true progression. AUC 0.80 is achieved. This is a treatment response classification task in diagnosed GBM, not initial tumour type detection.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, pseudo-progression, GBM, treatment response, post-chemoradiation MRI
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, 540 post-treatment MRI, AUC 0.80, pseudo-progression classification — treatment response not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for pseudo-progression vs true progression classification AUC 0.80, treatment response.

papers.title: Radiomic Signature for Predicting Post-Surgical GBM Recurrence Location from Pre-Operative MRI
papers.abstract: Tumour recurrence in GBM often occurs at the surgical resection margin. We extract radiomic features from pre-operative MRI to predict recurrence location at 6 months post-surgery. This is a prognostic spatial prediction task after diagnosis; no tumour detection or classification is performed.
papers.topic_hint: radiomics, GBM recurrence location, prognostic, post-surgical
papers.methodology_hint: radiomics, recurrence location prediction, prognostic — not tumour detection or classification
papers.claimed_contribution: Radiomic signature for GBM recurrence location prediction, prognostic not classification.

papers.title: Survey of AI for Brain Tumour Imaging: 75 Papers from 2018-2023
papers.abstract: We survey 75 papers applying AI to brain tumour MRI covering detection, segmentation, grading, and treatment response. Benchmark comparisons and methodological trends are discussed. No new model is developed.
papers.topic_hint: survey, AI, brain tumour, MRI, 75 papers
papers.methodology_hint: survey, 75 papers, no new model
papers.claimed_contribution: Survey of 75 brain tumour AI papers 2018-2023, no new model.

papers.title: Dense CNN for Pituitary Adenoma Subtype Classification from MRI
papers.abstract: Pituitary adenoma subtypes differ in hormonal activity and surgical approach. We train a densely connected CNN on 680 pituitary MRI studies to classify functioning versus non-functioning adenomas and by size category. Macro-AUC 0.88 is achieved, outperforming a feature-based SVM baseline and endocrinologist assessment.
papers.topic_hint: dense CNN, pituitary adenoma, MRI, subtype classification
papers.methodology_hint: dense CNN, 680 pituitary MRI, macro-AUC 0.88, SVM baseline, endocrinologist comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: Dense CNN for pituitary adenoma subtype classification macro-AUC 0.88, 2 baselines.

papers.title: Bayesian Neural Network for Uncertainty-Aware Brain Tumour Type Classification
papers.abstract: Uncertain predictions in brain tumour classification should trigger radiologist review rather than automatic reporting. We train a Bayesian CNN using stochastic weight averaging on 1,600 brain MRI studies for four-class tumour type classification (GBM, LGG, meningioma, metastasis). Macro-AUC 0.93 is achieved with calibrated posteriors. Routing uncertain cases to expert review raises effective accuracy to 0.96 in simulation. Comparison against a deterministic ResNet and an ensemble baseline is provided.
papers.topic_hint: Bayesian CNN, brain tumour classification, uncertainty, 4-class, MRI
papers.methodology_hint: Bayesian CNN SWA, 1600 MRI, macro-AUC 0.93, ResNet and ensemble baselines, calibrated uncertainty
papers.claimed_contribution: Bayesian CNN for 4-class brain tumour classification macro-AUC 0.93, uncertainty routing, 2 baselines.
